rationale: This reaction shows the decarbonylation of a simple, acyclic aliphatic aldehyde (heptanal) to the corresponding alkane (heptane) using Wilkinson's catalyst. This is a classic textbook example that clearly illustrates the deformylation of an aldehyde to an alkane, which is the core transformation of the Tsuji-Wilkinson decarbonylation. The use of a simple, saturated alkyl chain avoids ambiguity with other reaction families.
collision_avoidance_note: The substrate is a simple aliphatic aldehyde, avoiding aromatic rings which could trigger hydrodeformylation or other arene-related reactions. It also avoids unsaturated systems (alkenes, alkynes) which could undergo hydroformylation or other additions. The transformation is a clear C-C bond cleavage (deformylation), distinct from decarboxylation (loss of CO2) or deoxygenation (e.g., Wolff-Kishner, Clemmensen).
